title: Ability to Archive Updates or Mark Updates as Complete
excerpt: It would be wonderful to have the ability to Archive updates on an item, or in some way mark them as complete or no longer needed so that you don’t have to keep scrolling past them, BUT they’re still there to refer back …

posts.author: remawhirter (Rachel Mawhirter)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be wonderful to have the ability to Archive updates on an item, or in some way mark them as complete or no longer needed so that you don’t have to keep scrolling past them, BUT they’re still there to refer back to or restore if need be. For projects that are meatier or require being passed back and forth between team members, some items end up with a lot of Updates on them. And many updates end up getting completed and don’t need to be “in the way” after they’re handled.

posts.author: tibor93 (Tibor Tasi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @remawhirter,

If some of the updates are actual tasks, maybe handle them in subtasks instead of updates?
This would also help you to monitor the regular tasks for a project and later on these subtasks could be aumatically generated with an automation.

What do you think?
Regards,
Tibor
